meta.notes: Power use case benchmark v2 — multi-page, multi-source, structured extraction tasks. Baseline used WebFetch+WebSearch only; Skill used Firecrawl scrape/search/extract. WebFetch permissions were denied for many agents, so baseline relied heavily on WebSearch.

baseline.notes: Got Vercel fully, Netlify via docs fallback, Render only partial from search snippets. WebFetch denied for netlify.com and render.com.

skill.notes: All 3 sites scraped in 1 parallel turn with JSON extraction. Full pricing tiers for all 3 providers. Minor gaps in Vercel's opaque usage-based model.

baseline.notes: 58 endpoints found. JS-rendered docs.github.com returned empty; fell back to Enterprise Server 3.13 mirror pages. Missing branches, commits, statuses sub-sections (404 on Enterprise).

skill.notes: 59 endpoints extracted from 8 sub-pages via JSON extraction with waitFor. JS-rendered pages handled cleanly. Accuracy improved — all endpoints verified against index ToC. A few endpoints missed by LLM extraction on dense pages.

baseline.notes: WebFetch denied for all 5 source URLs. Only snippet-level data from WebSearch. Got framework names and basic recommendations but no benchmark data or deep analysis.

skill.notes: Full articles scraped from GfG, Coderio, dev.to. Got ASGI benchmark numbers (Litestar 50k RPS, FastAPI 45k), serverless cold start data, detailed framework comparison matrix. Reddit blocked by Firecrawl.

baseline.notes: ~20 pages surfaced via WebSearch snippets. Got all major API categories and beta headers. Missing exact parameter schemas and response details.

skill.notes: 10 pages scraped directly. Got exact parameters, response schemas, rate limits. Some pages too large (100k+ chars) for inline parsing. firecrawl_map and firecrawl_crawl denied. Accuracy improved to 3/3 — data verified from source.

baseline.notes: Found matrix at github.com/pricing (not /features). Partial extraction — React-rendered checkmarks didn't render. 9 categories, most major rows captured.

skill.notes: Same matrix found at /pricing. React hydration 'Uh oh!' errors replaced dynamic check/X icons even with waitFor:8000. Reconstructed from surrounding copy. No improvement over baseline for this heavily JS-rendered grid.

baseline.notes: 7 sources from WebSearch snippets. Good synthesis of all 3 dimensions (performance, browser support, use cases). WebFetch denied — no full article reads.

skill.notes: MDN pages scraped directly — got exact browser version compatibility tables, protocol architecture details, performance characteristics. Stack Overflow benchmark data. Perfect score.

baseline.notes: WebFetch denied for all 3 sites. WebSearch with domain filtering gave solid feature lists from official sites. PlanetScale dual positioning (Vitess+Postgres) captured.

skill.notes: All 3 homepages scraped in 1 parallel turn. Accuracy improved — features directly from official pages. Completeness limited to homepage-level features; deeper /features pages would improve.

baseline.notes: 6 operations found with key parameters from search snippets. Missing deeper/Connect-only params (transfer_data, radar_options, on_behalf_of). WebFetch denied.

skill.notes: All 6 operations with complete parameter lists including Connect-only fields. Stripe renders all operations on one page — single markdown scrape captured everything. Deprecation notice correctly captured.

baseline.notes: All 5 releases extracted perfectly. nodejs.org is server-rendered — WebFetch handled it cleanly. Parallel fetches of individual release pages.

skill.notes: Same perfect score. Server-rendered pages work equally well with both tools. Firecrawl JSON extraction provided clean structured data. Concurrency throttling noted but all calls succeeded.

baseline.notes: 6+ sources per side from WebSearch snippets. Comprehensive synthesis with specific arguments. Stats (15% bug reduction, 60M weekly npm downloads) plausible but unverified — WebFetch denied.

skill.notes: firecrawl_search with scrapeOptions returned 112k-119k char results — too large to parse. Fell back to WebSearch. Same scores as baseline. Key finding: scrapeOptions overflow is a failure mode.
